hints:
Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(\blueD x+\maroonC 6)(\blueD x+\maroonC 6)
\\\\
&=(\blueD x+\maroonC 6)^2
\\\\
&=\blueD x^2+2(\blueD x)(\maroonC 6)+\maroonC 6^2
\\\\
&=x^2+12x+36
\end{align}$

We can expand the expression like we expand the product of any two binomials.

However, notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square." It can be expanded using this pattern:

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(\blueD x+\maroonC 7)^2
\\\\
&=\blueD x^2+2(\blueD x)(\maroonC 7)+\maroonC 7^2
\\\\
&=x^2+14x+49
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(\blueD x+\maroonC 2)(\blueD x+\maroonC 2)
\\\\
&=(\blueD x+\maroonC 2)^2
\\\\
&=\blueD x^2+2(\blueD x)(\maroonC 2)+\maroonC 2^2
\\\\
&=x^2+4x+4
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(\blueD{2x}+\maroonC 3)^2
\\\\
&=(\blueD{2x})^2+2(\blueD{2x})(\maroonC 3)+\maroonC 3^2
\\\\
&=4x^2+12x+9
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(\blueD{3x}+\maroonC 8)^2
\\\\
&=(\blueD{3x})^2+2(\blueD{3x})(\maroonC 8)+\maroonC 8^2
\\\\
&=9x^2+48x+64
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(\blueD{4x}+\maroonC 1)(\blueD{4x}+\maroonC 1)
\\\\
&=(\blueD{4x}+\maroonC 1)^2
\\\\
&=(\blueD{4x})^2+2(\blueD{4x})(\maroonC 1)+\maroonC 1^2
\\\\
&=16x^2+8x+1
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(x-5)^2
\\\\
&=\big(\blueD x+(\maroonC{-5})\big)^2
\\\\
&=\blueD x^2+2(\blueD x)(\maroonC{-5})+(\maroonC{-5})^2
\\\\
&=x^2-10x+25
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(x-2)^2
\\\\
&=\big(\blueD x+(\maroonC{-2})\big)^2
\\\\
&=\blueD x^2+2(\blueD x)(\maroonC{-2})+(\maroonC{-2})^2
\\\\
&=x^2-4x+4
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(x-10)(x-10)
\\\\
&=(x-10)^2
\\\\
&=\big(\blueD x+(\maroonC{-10})\big)^2
\\\\
&=\blueD x^2+2(\blueD x)(\maroonC{-10})+(\maroonC{-10})^2
\\\\
&=x^2-20x+100
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\big(\blueD{5x}+(\maroonC{-6})\big)^2
\\\\
&=(\blueD{5x})^2+2(\blueD{5x})(\maroonC{-6})+(\maroonC{-6})^2
\\\\
&=25x^2-60x+36
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(7x-1)^2
\\\\
&=\big(\blueD{7x}+(\maroonC{-1})\big)^2
\\\\
&=(\blueD{7x})^2+2(\blueD{7x})(\maroonC{-1})+(\maroonC{-1})^2
\\\\
&=49x^2-14x+1
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a+b)=(a+b)^2$

This form is what we call "a perfect square":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)^2=\blueD a^2+2\blueD a\maroonC b+\maroonC b^2$

Let's use the above pattern, where $\maroonC b=\maroonC{-3}$, because we are dealing with the perfect square of a subtraction.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(2x-3)(2x-3)
\\\\
&=(2x-3)^2
\\\\
&=\big(\blueD{2x}+(\maroonC{-3})\big)^2
\\\\
&=(\blueD{2x})^2+2(\blueD{2x})(\maroonC{-3})+(\maroonC{-3})^2
\\\\
&=4x^2-12x+9
\end{align}$
